015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.sse;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.io.*;
022import java.time.*;
023import java.util.concurrent.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.marshall.sse.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.util.*;
028
029/**
030 * Fluent SSE response helper.
031 */
032public class SseResponseSupport implements AutoCloseable {
033
034   private final RestResponse response;
035   @SuppressWarnings({
036      "resource" // Writer is response-owned and intentionally not closed by this wrapper.
037   })
038   private final FinishablePrintWriter writer;
039   @SuppressWarnings({
040      "resource" // Scheduler is BeanStore-managed and shared; this wrapper must not close it.
041   })
042   private final ScheduledExecutorService scheduler;
043   @SuppressWarnings({
044      "resource" // Heartbeat lifecycle is controlled by this wrapper and closed in close()/heartbeat().
045   })
046   private SseHeartbeat heartbeat;
047
048   /**
049    * Constructor.
050    *
051    * @param response The REST response.
052    * @throws IOException If the writer could not be created.
053    */
054   @SuppressWarnings({
055      "resource" // BeanStore returns container-managed scheduler reference; this wrapper borrows it.
056   })
057   public SseResponseSupport(RestResponse response) throws IOException {
058      this.response = assertArgNotNull("response", response);
059      this.scheduler = response.getContext().getBeanStore().getBean(ScheduledExecutorService.class).orElse(null);
060      response.setContentType("text/event-stream");
061      response.setHeader("Cache-Control", "no-cache");
062      response.setHeader("X-Content-Type-Options", "nosniff");
063      response.setHeader("Content-Encoding", "identity");
064      writer = response.getNegotiatedWriter();
065   }
066
067   /**
068    * Starts periodic heartbeat comments.
069    *
070    * @param interval The heartbeat interval.
071    * @return This object.
072    */
073   public SseResponseSupport heartbeat(Duration interval) {
074      if (scheduler != null) {
075         if (heartbeat != null)
076            heartbeat.close();
077         heartbeat = SseHeartbeat.start(scheduler, writer, interval);
078      }
079      return this;
080   }
081
082   /**
083    * Sends an SSE event.
084    *
085    * @param event The event.
086    * @return This object.
087    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurred.
088    */
089   public SseResponseSupport sendEvent(SseEvent event) throws IOException {
090      SseSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(event, writer);
091      return this;
092   }
093
094   /**
095    * Sends an SSE event from name+data values.
096    *
097    * @param name The event name.
098    * @param data The event data.
099    * @return This object.
100    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurred.
101    */
102   @SuppressWarnings({
103      "resource" // SSE stream resource lifecycle is managed by the servlet container.
104   })
105   public SseResponseSupport sendEvent(String name, Object data) throws IOException {
106      return sendEvent(new SseEvent(name, data == null ? null : data.toString()));
107   }
108
109   /**
110    * Sends a heartbeat/comment line.
111    *
112    * @param value The comment value.
113    * @return This object.
114    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurred.
115    */
116   public SseResponseSupport comment(String value) throws IOException {
117      SseSerializer.writeComment(writer, value);
118      return this;
119   }
120
121   /**
122    * Flushes pending output.
123    *
124    * @return This object.
125    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurred.
126    */
127   public SseResponseSupport flush() throws IOException {
128      writer.flush();
129      response.flushBuffer();
130      return this;
131   }
132
133   /**
134    * Drains a subscription until disconnect or interruption.
135    *
136    * @param subscription The subscription.
137    * @return This object.
138    * @throws IOException If an I/O error occurred.
139    */
140   @SuppressWarnings({
141      "resource" // Subscription lifecycle is handled in finally and by caller contract.
142   })
143   public SseResponseSupport sendFrom(SseSubscription subscription) throws IOException {
144      assertArgNotNull("subscription", subscription);
145      try {
146         while (! subscription.isClosed()) {
147            sendEvent(subscription.take());
148            flush();
149         }
150      } catch (InterruptedException e) {
151         Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
152      } finally {
153         subscription.close();
154      }
155      return this;
156   }
157
158   @Override /* AutoCloseable */
159   public void close() {
160      if (heartbeat != null)
161         heartbeat.close();
162   }
163}
